skill.name: datamol
skill.description: Use Datamol to guide Python cheminformatics workflows with RDKit-backed molecule parsing, standardization, descriptors, fingerprints, clustering, conformers, visualization, reactions, and molecular file I/O.
skill.summary: Guide Datamol workflows for practical RDKit-based cheminformatics and molecular data analysis.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but manual review found Markdown inline code, Python examples, chemistry terminology, and API names rather than executable Ruby backticks or cryptographic implementations. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The remaining concern is low-risk guidance for local and remote molecular file I/O, which should be used only with trusted paths and datasets.

low_findings.title: Legitimate local and remote molecular file I/O
low_findings.description: The skill documents reading and writing SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, image, S3, and HTTPS paths. This is expected for Datamol workflows, but users should avoid untrusted remote sources and protect private datasets when writing outputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly shows supported file and remote path examples. The behavior is legitimate for the stated data-processing purpose, so the risk is limited to user-controlled data handling.

low_findings.title: Static command and cryptography alerts dismissed as documentation false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography alerts occur in Markdown headings, inline API names, Python examples, and chemistry terms such as fingerprints and InChI keys. No evidence found of executable shell commands, credential access, obfuscation, or cryptographic code in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are readable Markdown documentation and Python usage examples, not Ruby or shell execution contexts. Targeted searches found no prompt injection, subprocess calls, eval usage, secrets, or credential exfiltration patterns.

content.value_statement: Cheminformatics workflows can be difficult when RDKit APIs require detailed setup. This skill guides Datamol patterns for parsing, cleaning, analyzing, clustering, visualizing, and exporting molecular datasets.
actual_capabilities: Guides SMILES, InChI, SELFIES, and RDKit molecule conversion with Datamol defaults.
Explains molecule sanitization, standardization, hydrogen handling, and failed-parse checks.
Covers descriptors, fingerprints, similarity distances, clustering, and diversity selection.
Documents scaffold extraction, BRICS and RECAP fragments, reactions, and toy datasets.
Shows Datamol file I/O for SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON, images, and remote paths.
Provides patterns for 3D conformers, SASA, molecular visualization, SAR analysis, and virtual screening.
limitations: It is a guidance skill and does not install Datamol, RDKit, or optional cloud storage packages.
Advanced RDKit control may require direct RDKit APIs outside the documented Datamol wrappers.
Large clustering jobs can be memory intensive because some methods build full distance matrices.
Remote file examples require trusted paths and properly configured storage credentials.

use_cases.title: Prepare compound libraries
use_cases.description: Load SDF or SMILES files, standardize molecules, remove invalid structures, and export clean datasets.

use_cases.title: Build screening workflows
use_cases.description: Generate fingerprints, compute similarity, rank virtual screening hits, and visualize selected compounds.

use_cases.title: Create molecular ML features
use_cases.description: Compute descriptors, scaffold splits, and fingerprint arrays for model training and evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Parse and clean molecules
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small SMILES list.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Datamol to parse this SMILES list, standardize each molecule, skip invalid entries, and explain the checks I should run.

prompt_templates.title: Compute descriptor table
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Datamol workflow that loads compounds from an SDF file, computes common descriptors, filters by drug-likeness, and saves the result.

prompt_templates.title: Cluster and select diversity
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced selection workflow for a compound library.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Datamol plan to fingerprint 1,000 molecules, cluster them by similarity, select diverse representatives, and visualize the selected set.

prompt_templates.title: Build virtual screening pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Datamol to outline a virtual screening pipeline with query actives, library fingerprints, similarity ranking, scaffold review, and output reporting.

output_examples.input: A researcher asks how to clean a mixed SMILES file before descriptor calculation.
output_examples.output: The response outlines parsing, standardization, invalid molecule removal, descriptor computation, and export checks for a clean molecular table.

output_examples.input: A data scientist needs representative compounds from a library.
output_examples.output: The response recommends fingerprints, similarity distances, clustering limits, diverse picking, and a visualization summary of selected molecules.

output_examples.input: A chemist wants to compare analogs in a SAR series.
output_examples.output: The response describes scaffold grouping, aligned molecular depictions, activity labels, and careful handling of missing or invalid molecules.

best_practices: Standardize molecules from external sources before descriptors, fingerprints, clustering, or model training.
Check for failed molecule parsing and handle missing structures before batch processing.
Use parallel processing and progress indicators for large descriptor, similarity, and conformer jobs.
anti_patterns: Do not run full distance-matrix clustering on very large libraries without checking memory needs.
Do not assume invalid SMILES strings become usable molecules without explicit validation.
Do not read or write remote molecular datasets unless the storage path and credentials are trusted.

faq.question: What is Datamol used for?
faq.answer: Datamol simplifies common RDKit cheminformatics tasks such as molecule conversion, standardization, descriptors, fingerprints, clustering, visualization, and file I/O.

faq.question: Does this skill replace RDKit?
faq.answer: No. Datamol wraps RDKit for common workflows, while advanced custom chemistry logic may still need direct RDKit APIs.

faq.question: Can it process SDF and SMILES files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents Datamol readers and writers for SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON, and related formats.

faq.question: Can it work with cloud storage?
faq.answer: Yes. Datamol supports remote paths through fsspec, but users should only use trusted storage locations and credentials.

faq.question: What size libraries are best for clustering?
faq.answer: Butina clustering is best for smaller libraries because it builds a full distance matrix. Diversity selection is better for larger sets.

faq.question: Does the skill execute code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Any execution depends on the user environment and installed Python packages.
